pass_1.bdr_summary: TwoFaced - Solo founders submit startup ideas via text input. Claude advocates for the idea while ChatGPT attacks it. System synthesizes structured verdict with strengths, flaws, and recommendation. Pay-per-analysis model with 60-second turnaround.

economics_snapshot.target_customer: Technical solo founders aged 35-55 building side projects
economics_snapshot.acquisition_channel: Direct marketing to existing ChatGPT/Claude users

questions_for_hero: What sample startup ideas should we use for manual testing?
How will you validate prompt quality without real API calls?
What constitutes a good vs bad analysis for initial testing?

pass_2.approved_bdr: TwoFaced - Manual adversarial validation tool. User pastes startup idea into form. Two hardcoded response templates simulate advocate vs adversary positions. Manual synthesis produces structured verdict. Static demo with mock Stripe flow.
pass_2.hld_document: Single HTML page with textarea input. Hardcoded response templates for 5-10 common startup types. Manual template selection based on input keywords. Static verdict generation using predefined structure. Mock Stripe checkout button with success page. No database, no real API calls, no user accounts.
engineering_questions: Which 5-10 startup archetypes need hardcoded templates?
How detailed should mock responses be for proof of concept?
What verdict structure fields are essential vs nice-to-have?

pass_3.qa_hlt_document: Test manual form submission with various startup idea lengths and types. Verify template matching logic works for common cases. Validate verdict structure completeness. Test mock payment flow end-to-end. Confirm 60-second target with manual template selection.

test_vectors.description: Submit marketplace startup idea, verify advocate template selection
Submit SaaS tool idea, verify adversary template selection
Test verdict synthesis with conflicting advocate/adversary outputs
Complete mock payment flow from submission to success page

risk_register.description: Hardcoded templates may not match submitted startup types
Manual template selection may exceed 60-second target
Mock payment flow may not represent real Stripe complexity

qa_questions_for_engineering: How will template matching handle edge cases and unusual startup types?
What fallback exists if no template matches the input?
How realistic should the mock payment integration be?

combined_task_list.description: Create HTML form with textarea for startup idea input
Build template matching logic based on keywords
Create verdict synthesis engine with structured output
Add mock Stripe checkout button and success page
Test all template combinations with sample inputs
Daily progress check - template quality review
Daily progress check - verdict structure feedback

pass_5.proposal_document_for_hero: TwoFaced Tier 1 Proof Slice: 8-day manual validation of adversarial AI concept. Hardcoded templates simulate advocate vs adversary analysis. Static verdict generation proves core value loop. Mock payment flow validates economic model. Zero integrations, manual template selection only. Delivers working demo to test user response before API investment.

hero_questions: Should we focus on 5 startup archetypes or expand to 10 for better coverage?
How detailed should the mock Stripe integration be for realistic testing?
What success criteria will determine if we proceed to Tier 2 with real APIs?
